_README: Mixed-DB deployment sample — same DBMS, two PostgreSQL databases. Copy the relevant 'ConnectionStrings' fragment into your environment-specific appsettings file (NOT the default appsettings.json — that one defaults to single-DB so local development still works out of the box). See docs/deployment-mixed-db.md for the deployment topology spectrum and migration boundaries.
_NOTE_ConnectionStrings: Two connection strings are mandatory regardless of topology because PgvectorRagDbContext.ConnectionStringName == 'PaperbaseRag' (no fallback to 'Paperbase'). For single-DB topology both values point at the same instance; for cross-DB topology they point at different instances. The two contexts maintain INDEPENDENT migration history tables ('__EFMigrationsHistory' for the main DB, '__EFMigrationsHistory_PgvectorRag' for the vector DB) — see PgvectorRagDbProperties.MigrationsHistoryTableName.
_NOTE_VectorDB: The 'PaperbaseRag' DB needs the pgvector extension installed (CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS vector;). Migrations create the HNSW index, but the extension itself must be present at migrate time. Pool size is typically tuned higher than the main DB because embedding pipelines fan out concurrent INSERT/SELECT against the vector table.
_NOTE_BackupAndRetention: Vector DB can be re-derived from main-DB content (re-run DocumentEmbeddingBackgroundJob for each document). Backup policy may therefore differ — vector DB can have a shorter PITR window than the main DB, accepting a re-embedding cost on disaster recovery in exchange for storage savings.
_NOTE_Reconciliation: Cross-DB cleanup uses OnCompleted + Begin(requiresNew: true) (after-commit semantics, not 2PC). Crashes between main-DB commit and vector-DB cleanup leave orphaned chunks/DocumentVector rows; PgvectorReconciliationBackgroundJob (in Rag.Pgvector.EntityFrameworkCore) is the safety net — schedule it periodically (e.g. nightly).
_NOTE_NotForCrossDBMS: This sample covers PostgreSQL-main + PostgreSQL-vector ONLY. Mixing main DB on SQL Server with PostgreSQL vector DB also requires per-provider migrations for the main DbContext (NOT zero-code) — see the 'Mixed DBMS' section in docs/deployment-mixed-db.md.
